of targeted selector configurations."}},"targetSenders()":{"returns":{"targetedSenders_":"The list of targeted sender addresses."}},"test_arbitrary_send_eth_el_dispatcher()":{"details":"The deployed EL Dispatcher (impl) at      Mainnet: DeployedAddresses.MAINNET_EXECUTION_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xca4DD914fA713214844c84F153A5e1627536a7fC`)      Holesky: DeployedAddresses.TESTNET_EXECUTION_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xa69dDEBd0B6893A6F3d34A5df610d0E2ED433D18`)      performs ETH transfers without zero-address validation,      allowing permanent ETH burn to address(0)."},"test_arbitrary_send_eth_operator_zero()":{"details":"The deployed CL Dispatcher (impl) at      Mainnet: DeployedAddresses.MAINNET_CONSENSUS_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0x462Dd07A79e5DDfBe0C171449C5c01788d5d03C3`)      Holesky: DeployedAddresses.TESTNET_CONSENSUS_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xD36B422a7EE65219732724d849B8b6BceD6155Fe`)      performs the operator fee transfer without zero-address validation."},"test_arbitrary_send_eth_withdrawer_zero()":{"details":"This vulnerability exists in the deployed code at:      Mainnet CL Dispatcher: DeployedAddresses.MAINNET_CONSENSUS_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0x462Dd07A79e5DDfBe0C171449C5c01788d5d03C3`)      Holesky CL Dispatcher: DeployedAddresses.TESTNET_CONSENSUS_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xD36B422a7EE65219732724d849B8b6BceD6155Fe`)      Both proxies point to the same implementation bytecode."},"test_el_treasury_zero_burns_eth()":{"details":"The deployed EL Dispatcher (impl) at      Mainnet: DeployedAddresses.MAINNET_EXECUTION_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xca4DD914fA713214844c84F153A5e1627536a7fC`)      Holesky: DeployedAddresses.TESTNET_EXECUTION_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xa69dDEBd0B6893A6F3d34A5df610d0E2ED433D18`)      sends treasury fee via .call{value: ...}(\"\") without checking      for address(0), burning the treasury share permanently."},"test_reentrancy_event_ordering()":{"details":"The reentrant dispatch sends all remaining ETH to its own      recipients (withdrawer, treasury, operator) because dispatch()      computes fees on address(this).balance which has been reduced      by the withdrawer send. This is a structural issue: the      reentrant call always consumes 100% of the remaining balance.      vm.expectCall proves the reentrant dispatch call WAS made."},"test_reentrancy_toggle_before_send()":{"details":"We cannot use assertTrue on state after revert because EVM rolls      back ALL state changes in the reverted call, including the mock's      withdrawn mapping. vm.expectCall observes the call at runtime."}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"excludeArtifacts()":{"notice":"Returns artifact identifiers configured via `excludeArtifact`."},"excludeContracts()":{"notice":"Returns contract addresses configured via `excludeContract`."},"excludeSelectors()":{"notice":"Returns selector exclusions configured via `excludeSelector`."},"excludeSenders()":{"notice":"Returns senders configured via `excludeSender`."},"failed()":{"notice":"Returns true if any test assertion has failed."},"setUp()":{"notice":"Sets up the test environment with real dispatcher contracts"},"targetArtifactSelectors()":{"notice":"Returns artifact-selector targets configured via `targetArtifactSelector`."},"targetArtifacts()":{"notice":"Returns artifact identifiers configured via `targetArtifact`."},"targetContracts()":{"notice":"Returns contract addresses configured via `targetContract`."},"targetInterfaces()":{"notice":"Returns address-interface targets configured via `targetInterface`."},"targetSelectors()":{"notice":"Returns selector targets configured via `targetSelector`."},"targetSenders()":{"notice":"Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist)."},"test_arbitrary_send_eth_el_dispatcher()":{"notice":"Same vulnerability exists in ExecutionLayerFeeDispatcher."},"test_arbitrary_send_eth_operator_zero()":{"notice":"If operator fee recipient is address(0), operator's fee is burned.         NOTE: treasury receives globalFee - operatorFee, NOT the full globalFee.         The operatorFee is computed from globalFee and sent to address(0) separately."},"test_arbitrary_send_eth_withdrawer_zero()":{"notice":"If the staking contract returns address(0) for the withdrawer,         the withdrawer's share is sent to address(0) via low-level call,         which succeeds silently and burns the ETH."},"test_cross_contract_admin_control()":{"notice":"The staking contract admin can change fee parameters at any         time, affecting all future dispatches."},"test_el_dispatcher_no_state_change_before_send()":{"notice":"Unlike CL dispatcher, EL dispatcher does not call         toggleWithdrawnFromPublicKeyRoot, so the reentrancy         risk is lower but still present (event ordering)."},"test_fee_recipient_eth_lock_no_dispatcher()":{"notice":"If dispatcher is address(0), eth cannot be recovered because         the FeeRecipient has no rescue/owner function."},"test_fee_recipient_init_frontrunning()":{"notice":"init() can only be called once. If front-run, the legitimate         caller's init reverts and the FeeRecipient has a malicious config."},"test_fee_recipient_no_setter()":{"notice":"FeeRecipient has no setDispatcher() or rescue function."},"test_fee_recipient_receive_direct_eth()":{"notice":"FeeRecipient forwards ETH correctly via withdraw() -> dispatcher."},"test_incorrect_equality_dust_griefing()":{"notice":"balance == 0 is a strict equality check. A dust amount (1 wei)         passes the check, but results in zero fees due to truncation."},"test_incorrect_equality_status_checks()":{"notice":"status == false checks are functionally correct (equivalent to         !status) but slither flags them as code quality."},"test_precision_loss(uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Fuzz test: operatorFee is computed from already-truncated         globalFee, causing cascading precision loss."},"test_precision_loss_dust_edge_case()":{"notice":"Edge case where small balances cause fees to round to zero."},"test_reentrancy_event_ordering()":{"notice":"Events are emitted AFTER external calls (CEI violation).         A malicious withdrawer can reenter dispatch() with a different         pubkey root during the ETH transfer, causing event emissions         to interleave and potentially confuse off-chain monitors.Events are emitted AFTER external calls (CEI violation).         A malicious withdrawer can reenter dispatch() with a different         pubkey root during the ETH transfer, causing event emissions         to interleave and potentially confuse off-chain monitors.         NOTE: Treasury must NOT be the reentrancy detector \u2014 doing so         triggers cascading reentrancy where treasury funds re-enter         dispatch, consuming the remaining balance and causing         TreasuryReceiveError (see test_reentrancy_toggle_before_send         for CEI violation proof via vm.expectCall).CEI violation in CL dispatcher: events are emitted AFTER         external calls (line 111 after lines 95-109), and state         changes (toggleWithdrawn) happen BEFORE external calls         (line 81 before line 95). This ordering allows a malicious         withdrawer to reenter dispatch() during the ETH transfer,         causing the reentrant dispatch to consume the remaining         dispatcher balance. The outer dispatch then fails with         TreasuryReceiveError because no ETH remains for treasury."},"test_reentrancy_toggle_before_send()":{"notice":"CEI violation in CL dispatcher: toggleWithdrawnFromPublicKeyRoot         (line 81) is called BEFORE the ETH send (line 95). If the         withdrawer reverts, the toggle HAS already executed, but EVM         state rollback reverts the toggle too. We use vm.expectCall to         verify the toggle WAS invoked during execution, proving the CEI         pattern violation in the source code."},"test_unauthorized_dispatch()":{"notice":"dispatch() is public with no access control. By design, but         allows anyone to trigger fee distribution at any time."},"test_unauthorized_dispatch_zero_balance()":{"notice":"Zero-balance dispatch correctly reverts."}},"version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"test/FeeDispatcherAudit.t.sol":"FeeDispatcherAuditTest"},"evmVersion":"london","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":10000},"remappings":[":@openzeppelin/=lib/@openzeppelin/",":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/"]},"sources":{"lib/forge-std/src/Base.sol":{"keccak256":"0x926f1c9907b7dadb56dc920ae80dc473070989ab1f09b63e207ddc2d37110faa","license":"MIT returns.excludedArtifacts_: The list of excluded artifact identifiers.

returns._0: True if any assertion has failed, false otherwise.

returns.targetedArtifactSelectors_: The list of targeted artifact-selector configurations.

returns.targetedArtifacts_: The list of targeted artifact identifiers.

returns.targetedContracts_: The list of targeted contract addresses.

returns.targetedSenders_: The list of targeted sender addresses.

test_arbitrary_send_eth_el_dispatcher().details: The deployed EL Dispatcher (impl) at      Mainnet: DeployedAddresses.MAINNET_EXECUTION_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xca4DD914fA713214844c84F153A5e1627536a7fC`)      Holesky: DeployedAddresses.TESTNET_EXECUTION_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xa69dDEBd0B6893A6F3d34A5df610d0E2ED433D18`)      performs ETH transfers without zero-address validation,      allowing permanent ETH burn to address(0).

test_arbitrary_send_eth_operator_zero().details: The deployed CL Dispatcher (impl) at      Mainnet: DeployedAddresses.MAINNET_CONSENSUS_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0x462Dd07A79e5DDfBe0C171449C5c01788d5d03C3`)      Holesky: DeployedAddresses.TESTNET_CONSENSUS_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xD36B422a7EE65219732724d849B8b6BceD6155Fe`)      performs the operator fee transfer without zero-address validation.

test_arbitrary_send_eth_withdrawer_zero(.details: This vulnerability exists in the deployed code at:      Mainnet CL Dispatcher: DeployedAddresses.MAINNET_CONSENSUS_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0x462Dd07A79e5DDfBe0C171449C5c01788d5d03C3`)      Holesky CL Dispatcher: DeployedAddresses.TESTNET_CONSENSUS_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xD36B422a7EE65219732724d849B8b6BceD6155Fe`)      Both proxies point to the same implementation bytecode.

test_el_treasury_zero_burns_eth().details: The deployed EL Dispatcher (impl) at      Mainnet: DeployedAddresses.MAINNET_EXECUTION_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xca4DD914fA713214844c84F153A5e1627536a7fC`)      Holesky: DeployedAddresses.TESTNET_EXECUTION_LAYER_FEE_DISPATCHER        (`0xa69dDEBd0B6893A6F3d34A5df610d0E2ED433D18`)      sends treasury fee via .call{value: ...}("") without checking      for address(0), burning the treasury share permanently.

test_reentrancy_event_ordering().details: The reentrant dispatch sends all remaining ETH to its own      recipients (withdrawer, treasury, operator) because dispatch()      computes fees on address(this).balance which has been reduced      by the withdrawer send. This is a structural issue: the      reentrant call always consumes 100% of the remaining balance.      vm.expectCall proves the reentrant dispatch call WAS made.

test_reentrancy_toggle_before_send().details: We cannot use assertTrue on state after revert because EVM rolls      back ALL state changes in the reverted call, including the mock's      withdrawn mapping. vm.expectCall observes the call at runtime.

failed().notice: Returns true if any test assertion has failed.

setUp().notice: Sets up the test environment with real dispatcher contracts

targetSenders().notice: Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist).

test_arbitrary_send_eth_operator_zero().notice: If operator fee recipient is address(0), operator's fee is burned.         NOTE: treasury receives globalFee - operatorFee, NOT the full globalFee.         The operatorFee is computed from globalFee and sent to address(0) separately.

test_arbitrary_send_eth_withdrawer_zero(.notice: If the staking contract returns address(0) for the withdrawer,         the withdrawer's share is sent to address(0) via low-level call,         which succeeds silently and burns the ETH.

test_cross_contract_admin_control().notice: The staking contract admin can change fee parameters at any         time, affecting all future dispatches.

test_el_dispatcher_no_state_change_befor.notice: Unlike CL dispatcher, EL dispatcher does not call         toggleWithdrawnFromPublicKeyRoot, so the reentrancy         risk is lower but still present (event ordering).

test_fee_recipient_eth_lock_no_dispatche.notice: If dispatcher is address(0), eth cannot be recovered because         the FeeRecipient has no rescue/owner function.

test_fee_recipient_init_frontrunning().notice: init() can only be called once. If front-run, the legitimate         caller's init reverts and the FeeRecipient has a malicious config.

test_fee_recipient_no_setter().notice: FeeRecipient has no setDispatcher() or rescue function.

test_fee_recipient_receive_direct_eth().notice: FeeRecipient forwards ETH correctly via withdraw() -> dispatcher.

test_incorrect_equality_dust_griefing().notice: balance == 0 is a strict equality check. A dust amount (1 wei)         passes the check, but results in zero fees due to truncation.

test_incorrect_equality_status_checks().notice: status == false checks are functionally correct (equivalent to         !status) but slither flags them as code quality.

test_precision_loss(uint256,uint256,uint.notice: Fuzz test: operatorFee is computed from already-truncated         globalFee, causing cascading precision loss.

test_precision_loss_dust_edge_case().notice: Edge case where small balances cause fees to round to zero.

test_reentrancy_event_ordering().notice: Events are emitted AFTER external calls (CEI violation).         A malicious withdrawer can reenter dispatch() with a different         pubkey root during the ETH transfer, causing event emissions         to interleave and potentially confuse off-chain monitors.Events are emitted AFTER external calls (CEI violation).         A malicious withdrawer can reenter dispatch() with a different         pubkey root during the ETH transfer, causing event emissions         to interleave and potentially confuse off-chain monitors.         NOTE: Treasury must NOT be the reentrancy detector — doing so         triggers cascading reentrancy where treasury funds re-enter         dispatch, consuming the remaining balance and causing         TreasuryReceiveError (see test_reentrancy_toggle_before_send         for CEI violation proof via vm.expectCall).CEI violation in CL dispatcher: events are emitted AFTER         external calls (line 111 after lines 95-109), and state         changes (toggleWithdrawn) happen BEFORE external calls         (line 81 before line 95). This ordering allows a malicious         withdrawer to reenter dispatch() during the ETH transfer,         causing the reentrant dispatch to consume the remaining         dispatcher balance. The outer dispatch then fails with         TreasuryReceiveError because no ETH remains for treasury.

test_reentrancy_toggle_before_send().notice: CEI violation in CL dispatcher: toggleWithdrawnFromPublicKeyRoot         (line 81) is called BEFORE the ETH send (line 95). If the         withdrawer reverts, the toggle HAS already executed, but EVM         state rollback reverts the toggle too. We use vm.expectCall to         verify the toggle WAS invoked during execution, proving the CEI         pattern violation in the source code.

test_unauthorized_dispatch().notice: dispatch() is public with no access control. By design, but         allows anyone to trigger fee distribution at any time.
